title: External race-state enrichment: safety car, weather, and traffic-gap features
summary: Real pit calls depend on more than tyre age: safety car/VSC periods, rain onset, and undercut traffic windows heavily alter stop timing.
rationale: Real pit calls depend on more than tyre age: safety car/VSC periods, rain onset, and undercut traffic windows heavily alter stop timing. OpenF1 and FastF1 expose race-control, weather, pit, and gap fields that map directly to these strategy triggers.
implementation_hint: Join race/session metadata by year-race-lap from OpenF1/FastF1: track status, safety car/VSC flags, rainfall/track temp, gap to leader, interval to car ahead, and recent pit activity. Create features like `under_vsc`, `laps_since_status_change`, `gap_ahead_small_for_undercut`, `field_pit_wave_last_2laps`, and weather-change deltas.
expected_effect: Most likely to help on exceptions where the current tyre-life heuristics fail: sudden caution periods, rain transitions, and traffic-driven undercuts/overcuts.
risk: External joins may be messy across seasons/naming conventions, and missing coverage could add noise if not validated race-by-race.
